source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/TimeSeries/ARIMAXAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- D5 LOOSE smoke oracle (self-computed from the ported C++ core, NOT yet emitter-verified; D6 tightens to exact). ARIMAXAnalysis drives a Bayesian DEMCzs MCMC over an ARIMAX(1,0,0) model with a Linear trend and no covariates, fit-only (forecasting_time_steps = 0, so the covariate forecast-tail extension is not exercised). training_time_steps pinned to 15; curve_length = 20. NOTE (D5): like ARIMA (see arima_analysis_smoke.json), this model's SHORT MCMC trajectory is chaotic across builds -- a floating-point reassociation flips an accept/reject so the point estimate / AIC / BIC / RMSE diverge between the C++ ctest and the R/Python package builds -- so this fixture asserts ONLY the build-stable STRUCTURAL invariants (curve length + the deterministic first-step forecast); the exact trajectory scalars are deferred to D6's controlled-build emitter oracle. thinning_interval pinned to 1. Short run (iterations 100, output_length 400).
